identity.short_name: Palindromic quintic real-root count -> orthogonal centralizer dimension for a T-built partition

problem_spec.summary: Composite (CHAIN) family: compute bridge quantity T from component A (Count (a,b) with P(1)=0 giving exactly four distinct real roots); then use that T as a required parameter in component B (Centralizer dimension of a tensor-sum nilpotent in so(35)) to determine the final output (reduced mod 1000). Instance summary: Use palindromic structure and the imposed root at 1 to factor a quintic and reduce real-root counting to a quadratic discriminant, yielding a count T; then form a partition from T and compute the orthogonal centralizer dimension via the conjugate-partition sum-of-squares formula, reduced mod 1000.
givens: T counts values of a for which P_a has exactly four distinct real roots.

parameter(s).name: bound_N
parameter(s).purpose: sets the finite integer search region for coefficients.

parameter(s).name: a
parameter(s).purpose: controls the symmetric coefficient pair on z^4 and z.

parameter(s).name: b
parameter(s).purpose: controls the symmetric coefficient pair on z^3 and z^2, then is constrained by P(1)=0.

parameter(s).name: tensor_pattern
parameter(s).purpose: determines the diagonal sl2-action and hence the Jordan partition of T

parameter(s).name: nilpotent_block_sizes
parameter(s).domain: single Jordan block in each factor
parameter(s).purpose: forces A,B to be principal nilpotents (raising operators)

parameter(s).name: T_bridge
parameter(s).domain: derived integer computed from component A invariants
parameter(s).purpose: interface quantity produced by component A and consumed by component B (non-stapled coupling).

invariant(s).name: palindromic_odd_degree_implies_minus_one_root
invariant(s).statement: For palindromic odd degree polynomials, x=-1 is always a root.

invariant(s).name: palindromic_reciprocal_form
invariant(s).statement: Coefficients satisfy coeff(z^k)=coeff(z^{5-k}) (pattern [1,a,b,b,a,1]), hence P(z)=z^5 P(1/z).

invariant(s).name: forced_root_at_minus_one
invariant(s).statement: P(-1)=0 for all (a,b), so z=-1 is always a root.

invariant(s).name: reciprocal_root_pairing
invariant(s).statement: If r ? 0 is a root of P, then 1/r is also a root with the same multiplicity. Hence real roots other than  1 occur as reciprocal pairs.

invariant(s).name: imposed_root_at_one
invariant(s).statement: The given constraint P(1)=0 is equivalent to a+b+1=0, so b=-a-1.

invariant(s).name: quadratic_in_t_after_substitution
invariant(s).statement: For z?0, letting t=z+1/z gives Q(z)=z^2 F(t), where F(t)=t^2+(a-1)t+(b-a-1).

invariant(s).name: explicit_factorization_of_F_under_P1_zero
invariant(s).statement: Under b=-a-1, F(2)=0 and by Vieta the other root equals b, so F(t)=(t-2)(t-b).

invariant(s).name: real_t_range_mapping
invariant(s).statement: For real z?0, t=z+1/z satisfies |t|=2. If |t|>2 then z+1/z=t has exactly two distinct real solutions r,1/r. If t=2 then z=1 (double); if t=-2 then z=-1 (double).

invariant(s).name: orthogonal_structure
invariant(s).statement: The symmetric non-degenerate forms on V and W induce a symmetric non-degenerate form Q on U=VW, and T lies in so(U,Q).

invariant(s).name: sl2_structure
invariant(s).statement: By Jacobson-Morozov, A and B are raising operators of sl2-triples, so V≅ and W≅ as sl2-modules; with the diagonal action on U, the raising operator is T.

invariant(s).name: jordan_partition_of_T
invariant(s).statement: Clebsch-Gordan gives U≅⊕⊕⊕⊕, hence the Jordan partition of T is λ=(11,9,7,5,3).

invariant(s).name: bridge_interface_invariant
invariant(s).statement: The composite is coupled by the interface variable T: component A uniquely determines T, and component B is defined/solved only after substituting this specific T into its parameter slot.

essential_assumption(s): Distinct real roots are counted without multiplicity; the (x-1)^2 factor contributes only one distinct root at 1.
a and b are integers constrained to a finite box.
The polynomial is palindromic of odd degree (ensuring reciprocity and the forced root z=-1).
The additional constraint P(1)=0 is imposed (so z=1 is a root and b=-a-1 holds).
Distinct real roots are counted without multiplicity.
Constant term is nonzero (prevents z=0 roots and supports the t=z+1/z reduction).
Field is C (characteristic 0), so JacobsonMorozov and complete reducibility of sl2-modules apply.
The invariant bilinear forms are symmetric (odd-dimensional irreps), so the sl2-actions land in so.
T is interpreted as the diagonal sl2 raising operator on U.
The bridge quantity T computed from component A is guaranteed to be an integer in the admissible range required by component B (e.g., as an index, bound, or group-action parameter).
uniqueness_certificate(s): The discriminant test uniquely determines which a contribute, hence T.
Given T, lambda is fixed and the centralizer formula yields a unique integer C.

Under P(1)=0, b=-a-1 and F(t)=(t-2)(t-b). The distinct real roots of P are {-1} ? (real roots of Q), and real roots of Q correspond exactly to t-roots of F with |t|=2 via t=z+1/z. Thus P has exactly four distinct real roots iff the second t-root b satisfies |b|>2, producing exactly one additional real reciprocal pair {r,1/r}, and no other t-roots exist.
The condition b=-a-1 and |b|>2 together with |a|,|b|=8 defines a finite set of integer pairs (a,b) uniquely, so the resulting count is uniquely determined.

The sl2-module structures of V and W are uniquely determined by the principal nilpotent hypotheses. ClebschGordan uniquely fixes the decomposition of U and thus the Jordan partition  of T. The orthogonal centralizer dimension is a deterministic function of , hence the answer is unique.
(N1 necessity) If T is not computed from component A, component B has multiple admissible outputs across different possible parameter choices; thus the composite is ill-posed without the bridge.
(N2 non-factorization) The final answer is not a trivial Combine(ansA, ansB): the computed T restricts/changes the admissible branch structure inside component B before its output is even defined.

technique(s).name: partition_centralizer_dimension_so
technique(s).purpose: compute C via conjugate partition squares minus odd-part correction

technique(s).name: symmetry_exploitation_via_reciprocity
technique(s).purpose: force the root z=-1 and constrain any non- 1 real roots to reciprocal pairs.

technique(s).name: impose_root_at_one_to_reduce_parameters
technique(s).purpose: convert the root constraint into a linear relation between integer parameters.

technique(s).name: translate_real_root_count_to_t_root_location
technique(s).purpose: decide when Q contributes an extra real reciprocal pair beyond z=1.

technique(s).name: integer_pair_counting_on_linear_slice
technique(s).purpose: count integer solutions on a line segment in the lattice.

technique(s).name: JacobsonMorozov sl2-triple construction
technique(s).purpose: realize A and B as sl2 raising operators on irreducible orthogonal modules

theorem(s).name: reciprocal_polynomial_root_pairing
theorem(s).role: In a reciprocal polynomial with nonzero constant term, roots occur in reciprocal pairs.

theorem(s).name: real_solution_criterion_for_z_plus_inverse
theorem(s).role: z+1/z=t has two distinct real solutions iff |t|>2; yields double solutions at t= 2.

theorem(s).name: vieta_formulas
theorem(s).role: Determines the second t-root of F once t=2 is known to be a root.

theorem(s).name: JacobsonMorozov theorem
theorem(s).role: embeds a nilpotent element into an sl2-triple

theorem(s).name: ClebschGordan rule for sl2
theorem(s).role: decomposes tensor products of sl2 irreducibles

theorem(s).name: C[t]-module centralizer formula
theorem(s).role: gives dim C_gl(T) = Σ (λ'_k)^2 for nilpotent Jordan type λ

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Use algebraic symmetry to constrain root structure (reciprocal pairing).
Use a symmetry-adapted substitution (t=z+1/z) to reduce degree and turn root counting into inequalities.
Jordan canonical form as a C[t]-module decomposition

critical_trick(s): Factor P_a using palindromicity plus the imposed root at 1, reducing to a quadratic.
Count admissible a by the quadratic discriminant sign.
Bridge the resulting count T into a partition and apply the orthogonal centralizer formula.
Bridge Lemma (explicit): compute T in component A, then substitute this specific T into component B; justify N1 and N2 to rule out stapling.

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: basic modular arithmetic and combinatorial counts

library(s).name: numpy
library(s).reason: fast numerical root computation to brute-force verify the derived criterion over all (a,b) in the box.

task(s).name: primary_structural_check
task(s).reason: Execute the intended structural route in a CAS-friendly way: compute bridge T from component A using symbolic identities or explicit enumeration, then compute component B output with the substituted T, finally reduce mod 1000; confirm matches the recorded Answer.

task(s).name: secondary_independent_check
task(s).reason: Independent cross-check: avoid reusing the same derivation as primary. Use a different route (e.g., brute-force enumeration over the finite search space, numeric root approximation with direct evaluation, or an alternate invariant/Burnside count) to recompute the final answer and compare.
